properties.title: Mountain Close, Exmouth
properties.body: Two-bedroom leasehold maisonette in a quiet residential cul-de-sac in Littleham, Exmouth (EX8 2PJ). Spacious lounge, fitted kitchen, two bedrooms upstairs, family bathroom, and a private balcony with outdoor seating. A small patch of land adjoining the property offers a rare chance to add proper outdoor space. 114 years left on the lease, £0 ground rent, council tax band A. Needs modernisation throughout.
properties.why: Jesse spotted this one himself while testing the family submission flow — it's the cheapest property on the whole list at £200,000, and it's genuinely in Exmouth (Littleham), close to the brothers. Worth a look for that price point alone, but it needs real scrutiny: parking isn't mentioned at all, it's leasehold, and the bedrooms being 'upstairs' raises a question mark over long-term ground-floor living.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A small patch of land adjoining the property that could potentially be annexed to create a proper garden — worth asking the agent/freeholder whether it's included or purchasable.

properties.aiTake: The cheapest thing on the list by a wide margin, and it's genuinely in Exmouth near the brothers — but the price reflects real gaps: no parking mentioned at all, needs modernising throughout, it's leasehold (114 years is fine, not a dealbreaker), and 'bedrooms upstairs' makes the ground-floor-long-term question uncertain. Worth a viewing to check parking and layout in person before ruling in or out — this is more a starter/investment-shaped property than a forever-home shape.

parking.note: Listing doesn't mention parking either way — no drive, garage, or space referenced anywhere in the description.

properties.title: Haynes Cottages, Lympstone

flags.text: HAS PARKING (3 cars, gravelled bay + electric sockets)

properties.body: Two-bedroom terraced cottage in Lympstone with a modern kitchen/diner, a ground-floor shower room, and a new gas boiler (Feb 2026). Sold with no onward chain. The standout feature is outside: a gravelled parking bay for up to 3 cars with outside electric sockets, a large private garden, a substantial timber cabin/studio (15'8" x 12'5") usable as an office or hobby space, a potting shed, and three further storage sheds.
properties.why: The best parking situation found in Lympstone all sweep, by a wide margin — most of the village's cottages have on-street parking only, and this one has a proper 3-car gravelled bay with electric hookups. Add a big garden, a genuine garden studio, and no chain, and this is a strong all-rounder in the village that arguably has the best location fit of anywhere on the list (estuary, own station, Woodbury Common all close by).

checklist.uniqueFeature: Timber cabin/studio (15'8"x12'5") in the garden plus a rare 3-car parking bay with electric sockets — most Lympstone cottages have none of this

properties.aiTake: The find of the sweep — real 3-car parking with electric hookups, a big garden, a garden studio, no chain, and comfortably under your real budget target. Only real caveat is the same one every Lympstone property carries: not especially close to either brother despite being near Exmouth itself.

parking.note: Gravelled parking bay for up to 3 cars with outside electric sockets — the best parking on the whole list.

properties.title: Longmeadow Road, Lympstone (Grade II 17th-century cottage)
properties.body: One-bedroom Grade II listed 17th-century cottage in Lympstone with exposed beams, an inglenook fireplace with a log-effect stove, and an ensuite. South-facing garden borders a spring-fed stream, with a detached oak-framed garden studio (power and water connected). Vendor has disclosed Japanese knotweed at the garden perimeter (spring 2026), now under a 10-year insurance-backed treatment guarantee.
properties.why: Genuine period character in the same top-tier village as Farleys and Haynes below, with a lovely bonus garden studio by a stream. The knotweed disclosure is a real negotiating point rather than a dealbreaker given the guarantee is already in place, but worth a solicitor/surveyor's eye. Only one bedroom and no real parking (on-street only) are the practical trade-offs.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Detached oak-framed garden studio beside a spring-fed stream

properties.aiTake: Genuine Grade II character with a lovely garden studio by a stream, but only one bedroom, no real parking, and a disclosed knotweed issue worth getting a surveyor's eye on before getting attached.

parking.note: No off-street parking; on-street only in Lympstone village.

properties.title: Brookside Cottage, Lympstone
properties.body: Two-bedroom freehold cottage in Lympstone, no onward chain, reduced to guide price £275,000. Open-plan sitting/dining/kitchen downstairs, first-floor bedrooms with exposed beams, wood burner. Currently run as a successful Airbnb. Rear courtyard with a shed rather than a full garden.
properties.why: Chain-free with a genuine rental track record if you wanted to keep letting it out, in the same desirable village as the other Lympstone finds this sweep. Parking isn't stated either way (listing says ask agent) and there's a courtyard rather than a real garden, so it's a notch below Haynes on practicality.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Currently run as a successful Airbnb — could continue as an income-generating let

properties.aiTake: No-chain cottage with a genuine rental track record as an Airbnb, but parking is unconfirmed and there's only a courtyard rather than a real garden.

parking.note: Listing says "ask agent" rather than confirming either way.

properties.title: Morcote, The Strand, Lympstone
properties.body: One-bedroom period cottage in the heart of Lympstone village, near the estuary. Solid wood flooring, an inset wood stove on a slate hearth, sash windows with shutters, solid wood doors with iron ironmongery. No garden or outside space at all. On-street parking only.
properties.why: Cheap and genuinely characterful, right in the middle of the village, but it's the weakest of this sweep's Lympstone finds on practicality — no garden whatsoever and no dedicated parking. Worth a look only if the interior character outweighs having zero outdoor space.
properties.aiTake: Cheap, characterful, and right in the heart of Lympstone, but zero outdoor space and street parking only make it the weakest of this sweep's Lympstone finds.

parking.note: No garden or off-street parking; on-street only, right in the village centre.

properties.title: High Street, East Budleigh (Grade II cottage)
properties.body: Double-fronted Grade II listed cottage on the High Street in East Budleigh village. Living room with fireplace, separate dining room, good-sized kitchen, two first-floor double bedrooms, spacious bathroom, cloakroom, feature ceiling beams throughout, enclosed rear garden, gas central heating.
properties.why: Not to be confused with the £435,000 East Budleigh thatched cottage already on file below (likely sold/withdrawn) — this is a separate, much cheaper Grade II cottage in the same village, well under budget with genuine period features (beams, fireplace). East Budleigh itself is inland of Budleigh Salterton by about 2 miles, still within easy reach of the coast. Checked the full listing text directly — genuinely no parking, garage, or space mentioned anywhere, not just an omission in the summary. On the street itself: High Street has limited on-street parking with a 30-minute free limit on one side, so it isn't realistic for a resident's own car; the practical option is a small free 17-space car park just off the High Street (2-hour limit) plus the larger Hayes Lane car park a short walk away. East Budleigh is a small village and doesn't appear to run a formal residents' permit scheme (those are typically only in larger towns like Exmouth/Sidmouth) — so day-to-day parking would likely mean walking a couple of minutes from a car park rather than permit-based street parking outside the door.
properties.aiTake: Solid character and price, but no parking, and the ocean/station/pool are all a drive away rather than walkable — a village-life trade rather than a coast-or-trail-adjacent one.

parking.note: No parking, garage, or space of any kind — confirmed directly from the full listing text. High Street has limited 30-min on-street parking; the practical option is a free 17-space car park just off the High Street (2-hr limit) or the larger Hayes Lane car park nearby. No formal residents' permit scheme in this small village.

properties.title: The Marles, Exmouth (ground-floor flat)

flags.text: PARKING: GARAGE TO RENT NEARBY (not included)

properties.body: Spacious purpose-built ground-floor flat undergoing extensive modernisation — new flooring, refurbished kitchen and shower room, full redecoration. Entrance hall with utilities cupboard, large lounge/diner, two double bedrooms, shower room/WC, surrounded by communal gardens. Leasehold with an extended lease granted on completion. Service charge £1,440/year.
properties.why: Passes the ground-floor test for a flat and is well under budget, but the parking is soft: the listing only says a garage is understood to be available to rent close by, not an allocated space that comes with the sale — weaker than Exeter Road or Douglas Avenue below, which both have confirmed allocated parking.
properties.aiTake: Ticks the practical boxes (ground floor, cheap, close to everything in Exmouth) but has none of the character you respond to, and the garage is only a maybe. Right at your £200k comfort line, which helps its case.

parking.note: A garage is understood to be available to rent nearby, but it's not included with the sale — not a guaranteed space.

properties.title: Farleys, The Strand, Lympstone (Grade II cottage)
properties.body: Two-bedroom Grade II listed cottage in the heart of Lympstone village — exposed beams and stonework throughout, cosy sitting/dining room, well-appointed kitchen, family bathroom, two loft areas with Velux windows, gas central heating. Freehold and chain-free. No garden and no dedicated parking — permit parking is available at the village car park nearby. Two pubs, shop, post office, café, sailing club and the estuary all on the doorstep.
properties.why: Location-wise this is arguably the best-placed find yet: Lympstone sits on the Exe estuary directly between Exmouth and Woodbury Common — water one way, trails the other, and one of your brothers is in Woodbury just up the hill. Genuine period character (Grade II, beams, stonework), chain-free, and £5k under ceiling. The catches: no parking (the standing hard requirement — waived here only because it's genuinely characterful, with village car park permits as the workaround), no outdoor space, and listed-building consent applies to any alterations. Found via the Exmouth Journal property pages, not the portals.
properties.aiTake: Best location fit yet — estuary, its own station, and Woodbury Common all on the doorstep — but no garden, no parking, and Grade II status likely rules out a ground-floor retrofit. One honest caveat: by your own read, Lympstone isn't especially close to either brother (Woodbury or Otterton) — worth it only if the deal/character is exceptional enough to outweigh that, which arguably it already is (Grade II, chain-free, £5k under budget). Your call. Also nudged down slightly for price — £295k is into the range you'd call over budget even though it's under the £300k hard ceiling.

research.question: Look further into what you actually do for parking on The Strand, Lympstone.
research.findings: The Strand itself has no dedicated resident bays — parking there is on-street with no confirmed residents' zone. Two car parks serve the immediate area: the free Lympstone Village Station car park right on The Strand (13 spaces, no permit needed, first-come-first-served, but can fill up with rail commuters), and Underhill Car Park a short walk away. Underhill's management has been unsettled for a while — East Devon District Council was in talks with Lympstone Parish Council years ago about the Parish taking it over and potentially issuing permits, and I couldn't confirm what, if anything, was finalised or whether a strict residents' scheme runs there now. Beyond that, East Devon District Council sells modular monthly/annual town permits for its pay-and-display car parks generally, but I couldn't confirm Lympstone is one of the towns those permits cover, nor find a price. Devon County Council handles on-street residents' permit zones separately and I couldn't confirm one exists for The Strand either. Bottom line: there's workable free/cheap parking nearby (the station car park is the best bet), but nothing deeded to the property and nothing guaranteed — worth a direct call to Lympstone Parish Council or East Devon's parking team before relying on it.

sources.label: Lympstone Village Website — Underhill Car Park
East Devon District Council — Car park permits
Devon County Council — Residents' parking permits

parking.note: No dedicated parking; nearest options are the free Lympstone Village Station car park (13 spaces, first-come-first-served) or Underhill Car Park nearby — nothing deeded to the property. See research findings below for the full picture.

properties.title: 3 Bell Street, Otterton
properties.body: Two-bedroom terraced cottage: sitting room, kitchen/breakfast room, ground-floor shower room, two first-floor bedrooms with a bathroom, enclosed rear garden with raised beds and a timber store. Roof renewed 2023 but the rest would benefit from general refurbishment. Freehold, Council Tax Band C, no onward chain.
properties.why: Cheapest confirmed in-budget option this sweep, and it's in the same village as the Otterton thatched cottage you already liked. But it's even more squarely the thing you flagged a reservation about there: village-centre, on-street parking only, not the remote/rural feel you said you'd prefer. Worth it only if the price and no-chain status outweigh that.
properties.aiTake: Cheapest confirmed option, but exactly the village-centre/no-real-parking profile you said you'd want to avoid if you have a choice.

parking.note: On-street parking only, in the same village as the Otterton thatched cottage.

properties.title: The Flexton, Ottery St Mary
properties.body: Deceptively spacious period cottage over three floors: ground-floor kitchen/dining room and a living room built around an open brick fireplace, bathroom with both bath and shower, three bedrooms upstairs (two doubles, one single). Private courtyard with gravel landscaping and pedestrian rear access. Near town-centre amenities, schools, and commuter links (A30, M5, London rail).
properties.why: Same price as the Bell Street cottage above but reads as more finished and genuinely characterful — an open brick fireplace and three floors of period detail rather than a fixer-upper. The trade-off: Ottery St Mary is inland, away from both the coast and Woodbury Common, and the courtyard access described sounds pedestrian-only, so parking is probably absent.
properties.aiTake: Most finished and characterful of the cheap options, but it's the one furthest from the coast/trails brief of anything on the list.

parking.note: Courtyard access described sounds pedestrian-only; parking isn't mentioned and seems unlikely given the access description.

properties.title: The Beacon, Exmouth (ground-floor flat)
properties.body: Two-double-bedroom ground-floor flat, a quarter-share of the freehold on a 999-year lease in a self-managed building of four apartments (£120/month maintenance). Bay-window sitting room overlooking the estuary, modern kitchen, bathroom with rainfall shower plus a separate cloakroom, stone fireplace. A secure parking space (up-and-over door) is included at the rear.
properties.why: Note: this is a different property from the over-budget Beacon house already on file below — same building name, unrelated listing. This one is genuinely ground-floor with a real allocated parking space (not just street parking) plus an estuary view, which is unusual for a flat at this price. But it's £9,950 (3%) over your £300k ceiling and it's a flat, so only worth a look if you'd flex the ceiling slightly for a genuine parking bay and the view.
properties.aiTake: Real ground-floor parking and an estuary view are rare for a flat, but it's over budget and still just a flat, not the characterful find you're really after. Already over the £300k hard ceiling, which the price alone makes hard to justify given your real ~£170-200k target.

parking.note: A secure allocated parking space (up-and-over door) is included at the rear — a real space, not just street parking.

properties.title: Cranford Avenue, Exmouth (ground-floor flat)
properties.body: Two-double-bedroom ground-floor apartment in the Avenues area of Exmouth, beautifully modernised throughout, sold with no onward chain and ready to move into.
properties.why: A safely-in-budget, ready-to-go fallback flat if nothing more characterful turns up — similar in spirit to the Douglas Avenue flat already on file, but £15,000 cheaper. No parking mentioned in the listing, so treat as unconfirmed rather than absent (Douglas Avenue has confirmed allocated parking).
properties.aiTake: Safe, ready-to-move-into fallback in the right town, but no character and parking is unconfirmed rather than guaranteed.

parking.note: Not mentioned in the listing either way.

properties.title: Circular Thatched Cliff-top Cottage, Peak Hill Road, Sidmouth
properties.body: A unique circular thatched house on a cliff-top position along the Jurassic Coast, designed in the 1960s for novelist R.F. Delderfield. Panoramic sea views over Ladram Bay, mature terraced gardens. Auction closes 21 July 2026 — just over two weeks out.
properties.why: Was Jesse's stated dream home, dropped 2026-07-06 once the erosion risk was actually looked into. Local press confirms the garden has already lost ground to a cliff fall, not just theoretical risk, and that stretch of Peak Hill has a well-documented run of landslips. Separately, standard UK buildings insurance excludes coastal erosion outright, there's no government backstop for it like there is for flooding, and lenders increasingly treat erosion-risk properties as unmortgageable — the only public money available is a £6,000 demolition grant if it's ever condemned, nothing toward lost value. That combination is why the guide price is so low, and why Jesse walked away. Kept here for the record rather than deleted.

research.question: What do the papers/local chatter say about the cliff collapse and why this house is going so cheap, before dismissing it entirely?
research.findings: The cottage lost part of its garden to a confirmed cliff fall in 2024 and now sits roughly 40ft (12m) from the edge of a 120m drop. The coastline here (Sidmouth Mudstone Formation, capped with Cretaceous Greensand at Peak Hill) is soft and fails unpredictably rather than eroding gradually — there's been a steady run of rockfalls along this stretch, including a large one in October 2024 and another as recently as late June 2026, serious enough that East Devon council has closed several nearby cliff-top paths as a precaution. A full cliff survey exists in the auction legal pack. All the press coverage traces back to the same wire story (Sidmouth Herald/Midweek Herald/Exmouth Journal all ran it) — none of it quotes an engineer, the council, or a neighbour on the specific risk, and no Facebook/community-forum chatter about this particular house turned up; it doesn't appear to be a locally-discussed listing. On whether stabilising the cliff (Jesse's caisson idea) is even feasible: no Peak-Hill-specific policy was found, but this is open, undeveloped cliff west of the harbour — the kind of coastline Shoreline Management Plans typically mark 'no active intervention', meaning little chance of public money or planning support for private hard defences. That's an inference from how these plans usually treat isolated clifftop properties, not a confirmed answer for this plot — worth a direct call to East Devon's coastal protection team before spending anything on engineering feasibility.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Circular 1960s design built for novelist R.F. Delderfield — architecturally one-of-a-kind

properties.aiTake: Character and privacy are off the charts, but declined for good reason — the erosion/insurance/mortgage problem is real, and it's the least practical for trains, pools, or trails of anything on the list.

parking.note: Not specifically checked — a cliff-top position suggests limited space, but this wasn't confirmed either way. Moot given the property was declined for erosion/insurance reasons.

properties.title: Thatched Cottage, The Green, Otterton
properties.body: Attractive three-bedroom thatched cottage right in the centre of Otterton village, close to the River Otter and Otterton Mill. South-facing rear garden, period stone fireplace and window seat. Needs modernising throughout — priced accordingly.
properties.why: Jesse flagged this one personally — near his brother, a lovely part of Devon. His one reservation: it's village-centre (on-street parking only) and he'd prefer something a bit more remote/rural. Worth weighing character + location against the more remote feel he's after. See 3 Bell Street above — a cheaper fixer-upper in the same village if this one's too finished/pricey.
properties.aiTake: The thatched-cottage character you love, near your brother, but it's the exact village-centre/on-street-parking trade-off you flagged not wanting — you said as much yourself.

parking.note: Village-centre position; on-street parking is the practical reality even though the listing doesn't confirm it explicitly.

properties.title: Barn at Whites Cottages, Feniton — AUCTION
properties.body: Freehold barn on a 0.21-acre plot on the edge of Feniton village. Already has full planning permission (East Devon DC ref 25/0370/FUL, granted Sept 2025) to convert and extend into a 3-bedroom dwelling with parking and garden. Going to online auction 23 July 2026.
properties.why: The self-build/conversion-plot opportunity Jesse said he'd consider — cheap land with permission already in hand. Downside: inland near Honiton, off the coast/trails brief.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Already has planning permission in hand — rare for this price

properties.aiTake: Cheapest, most flexible option — you could design in ground-floor living and a workshop from scratch — but it's inland, off the coast and trails brief entirely. Also comfortably under your real £170k target — the cheapest thing on the list by a wide margin.

parking.note: Parking is included in the already-granted planning permission for the converted dwelling.

properties.title: The Threshing Barn, Yettington
properties.linkLabel: exmouthjournal.co.uk (background coverage — agent's listing page no longer resolves)
properties.body: Two-bedroom barn conversion in the small hamlet of Yettington, directly on the walking/cycling routes linking Budleigh Salterton, East Budleigh, and Woodbury Common. Stable-style front door, vaulted ceilings, exposed beams, ~798 sq ft, gardens and allocated parking.
properties.why: About as close as a converted barn gets to the Woodbury Common trail network. Priced 25-33% over budget and compact for the money. Downgraded to likely sold this sweep: on top of the agent's own listing page redirecting to a generic search, a Rightmove mirror for a similar 3-bed Yettington barn conversion now also returns 410 Gone. Kept only as a benchmark/inspiration piece unless Jesse wants it chased further.
properties.aiTake: About the best trail-network location of anything found, and has real character, but likely gone and was already 25-33% over budget. Already well past the £300k ceiling — a benchmark piece, not a real contender on price alone.

parking.note: Gardens and allocated parking were confirmed in the original listing (property is likely sold).

properties.title: Douglas Avenue, Exmouth
properties.body: Two-bed purpose-built apartment, balcony, allocated parking, near seafront/town centre, between town and the sea.
properties.why: Conventional but solid fallback if nothing more characterful turns up in budget. It's a flat, floor level unconfirmed.
properties.aiTake: Solid, safe fallback with real parking in the right town, but no character and no garden — a placeholder rather than an exciting option.

parking.note: Allocated parking confirmed in the listing.

properties.title: The Beacon, Exmouth (inspiration only)
properties.body: Grade II-listed four-storey house needing renovation, sea views from the upper floors, private garden and garage, walking distance to town centre and seafront.
properties.why: Well over budget but kept as a benchmark for what full-renovation character stock costs locally.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Grade II four-storey renovation project with sea views

properties.aiTake: Character and garage boxes are both ticked hard, but it's 33%+ over budget and a full four-storey renovation project, not a quick move-in. Already well past the £300k ceiling, on top of being a major renovation project.

parking.note: Private garage included.

properties.title: Withycombe Village Road, Exmouth (former Methodist Church)
properties.price: Guide price £350,000 (as of first report, unconfirmed)
properties.body: Former Methodist Church and adjoining Church Hall, gardens, conversion project subject to planning consent.
properties.why: Couldn't re-locate this exact listing this sweep — may have sold or been withdrawn. Kept as inspiration for the flavour of conversion opportunity this market does throw up.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Former Methodist Church + Church Hall — a genuine conversion rarity

properties.aiTake: The most unusual conversion flavour found so far, but too much of the listing (price, status, layout) is unconfirmed to rate it with real confidence. Already well past the £300k ceiling on top of everything else unconfirmed.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way — the specific listing couldn't be relocated this sweep.

properties.title: Salterton Road, Exmouth (ground-floor retirement flat)
properties.body: Two-bedroom ground-floor retirement apartment, direct access to communal gardens, close to amenities and bus routes. Likely age-restricted.
properties.why: Cheapest option found originally; could not reconfirm this exact flat this sweep (a different Salterton Road retirement flat, Bronte Court, now shows instead). No photo included to avoid misattributing an image to the wrong flat.
properties.aiTake: Ticks ground-floor and location boxes cheaply, but likely age-restricted and has none of the character you're after — a fallback, not a favourite. Also under your real £170k target, which counts for more than the £300k ceiling implies.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way.

properties.title: Grade II Thatched Cottage, High Street, East Budleigh
properties.body: Genuinely characterful double-fronted thatched cottage, 1600s core with a 1700s rear extension.
properties.why: 45% over budget, kept as inspiration. The Rightmove listing now returns "410 Gone", suggesting it has sold or been withdrawn.
properties.aiTake: Genuinely characterful but 45% over budget and likely already gone — kept only as a benchmark. Already well past the £300k ceiling, on top of likely being sold.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way; listing has since gone (410).

properties.title: Clinton Garage, Budleigh Salterton — AUCTION
properties.body: Former commercial garage/MOT site right in Budleigh Salterton itself — conversion/redevelopment long-shot.
properties.why: Over budget and speculative, but flagged given the town-centre location and imminent auction date.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Former commercial garage/MOT site in the town centre — could plausibly be run as a hireable workshop, exactly the kind of oddball find you said you wanted flagged

properties.aiTake: The most literal 'workshop you could hire out' find yet, right in Budleigh itself, but it's speculative, over budget, and a redevelopment project rather than a move-in-ready home. Already well past the £300k ceiling on top of being speculative.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way — former commercial garage site, own parking situation unclear.

properties.title: The Coach House, Leslie Road, Exmouth
properties.body: Freehold, no parking mentioned — originally sent over by Jesse rather than found by search.
properties.why: Could not reconfirm this exact listing this sweep; a different, pricier Leslie Road property now shows instead. Worth a direct call: 01395 264111.
properties.aiTake: Could be a good, cheap, characterful find in the right town, but the listing couldn't be reconfirmed this sweep — worth a direct call before reading too much into it.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way — exact listing couldn't be relocated this sweep.

properties.title: Greenway, Woodbury (character cottage)
properties.body: Two-bedroom character cottage (527 sq ft) in a pedestrianised position in the heart of Woodbury village. Exposed ceiling beams and an inglenook fireplace with wood-burning stove, lounge/dining room, modern kitchen, first-floor bathroom, gas central heating. Front and rear gardens — the rear tiered across three levels with stone chippings, paving and two wood stores. Freehold, no chain.
properties.why: This is the first find actually IN Woodbury itself — meaning your brother is a short walk away and Woodbury Common's trails are effectively on the doorstep, which nothing else on the list can claim as directly. Genuine character (beams, inglenook, wood burner), real gardens, freehold, no chain, and £35k under the hard ceiling. The catch is the same village-centre trade-off as the Otterton/Bell Street/Flexton cottages: the listing doesn't mention parking at all, and a pedestrianised position makes that a real question mark rather than a formality. Found via a general portal search, not one of the usual agents.
properties.aiTake: Right in Woodbury itself, so closer to a brother and to the Common's trails than anything else tracked — but no parking mentioned in a pedestrianised spot, and no coast, train, or pool nearby.

parking.note: Pedestrianised position in the village centre makes off-street parking unlikely, though the listing doesn't confirm either way.

properties.title: Town Lane, Woodbury (period cottage)
properties.body: Double-fronted period cottage in Woodbury with a picturesque frontage and traditional features. Sitting room with fireplace, separate dining room, south-facing rear garden, and outbuildings for storage. Freehold, Council Tax Band A. Three bedrooms.
properties.why: A second genuine Woodbury find this sweep, right alongside Greenway — meaning a brother a short walk away and Woodbury Common's trails effectively on the doorstep. Real period character (fireplace, separate dining room) and storage outbuildings, but £15k pricier than Greenway and parking isn't mentioned at all.
properties.aiTake: Genuine Woodbury cottage character and close to a brother/the Common's trails, but no parking mentioned and pricier than the other Woodbury find on file — outbuildings for storage are a small consolation.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way.

properties.title: Woodbury (Conservation Area cottage)
properties.body: Double-fronted two-bedroom cottage in the Woodbury Conservation Area, with exposed beams, a wood-burning stove, and a modern kitchen with solid wood worktops. Enclosed courtyard garden. Freehold.
properties.why: A third Woodbury cottage now on file — this one specifically within the village's Conservation Area, with genuine beams and a wood burner. Only a courtyard rather than a full garden, and parking is unmentioned, same trade-off as the other two Woodbury finds.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Sits within the Woodbury Conservation Area

properties.aiTake: Conservation-area character with beams and a wood burner, close to a brother and the Common, but only a courtyard rather than a real garden and no parking mentioned.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way; only a courtyard garden is mentioned.

properties.title: Queen Street, Budleigh Salterton (former fisherman's cottage)
properties.body: Thoroughly modernised former fisherman's cottage, two bedrooms, two bathrooms. Sitting room with a bay window, new kitchen with bi-fold doors, en-suite, and an enclosed rear courtyard with an outside shower. Walking distance to the high street and beach.
properties.why: Genuinely characterful (a former fisherman's cottage) and a short walk from Budleigh's beach and high street — the most walkable-to-the-sea find of the sweep. On-street parking only and a courtyard rather than a real garden are the trade-offs.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Former fisherman's cottage with an outside shower off the courtyard

properties.aiTake: Nicely modernised former fisherman's cottage a short walk from Budleigh's beach and high street, but on-street parking only and just a courtyard, not a real garden.

parking.note: On-street parking only; walking distance to the high street and beach instead.

properties.title: Longbrook Lane, Lympstone (fixer-upper)
properties.body: Two-bedroom terraced cottage in Lympstone needing modernisation throughout — "put your own stamp on it." Utility room leading to a paved courtyard. Village location near the Exe Estuary with rail access.
properties.why: The cheapest Lympstone find this sweep — a genuine blank-canvas fixer-upper in the best-located village on the list (estuary, own station, Woodbury Common all close). Needs work throughout, parking is unconfirmed, and there's a courtyard rather than a garden.
properties.aiTake: Cheapest Lympstone option this sweep and a genuine fixer-upper opportunity in the best-located village on the list, but needs work throughout and parking is unconfirmed.

parking.note: Not confirmed either way; a paved courtyard is mentioned but no parking.

properties.title: All Saints Close, East Budleigh (modern terrace)
properties.body: Modern, low-maintenance three-bedroom terrace in East Budleigh with allocated off-street parking plus a visitor space. No onward chain.
properties.why: No period character to speak of, but genuine allocated parking plus a visitor space is unusual at this price point in the area, and it's chain-free and ready to go.
properties.aiTake: No character to speak of, but genuine allocated parking plus a visitor space is rare at this price point, and it's chain-free.

parking.note: Allocated off-street parking plus a visitor space.

properties.title: Vansittart Drive, Exmouth
properties.body: Plain two-bedroom terraced house in Exmouth with a conservatory and a garage in a nearby block. No onward chain.
properties.why: Nothing characterful about it, but it's a real garage (not just a maybe, unlike The Marles above), chain-free, and squarely in Exmouth near the brothers.
properties.aiTake: Plain and unexciting, but a real garage (not just a maybe), chain-free, and squarely in Exmouth near the brothers — a solid practical fallback.

parking.note: A real garage in a nearby block — not just a maybe.

properties.title: Norman Crescent, Budleigh Salterton
properties.body: 2004-built two-bedroom terrace in Budleigh Salterton with allocated off-road parking behind the property. En-suite to both bedrooms.
properties.why: Modern and characterless, but real allocated parking behind the property in a genuinely coastal town — a practical rather than exciting option.
properties.aiTake: Modern and characterless, but real allocated parking behind the property in a genuinely coastal town — a practical rather than exciting option.

parking.note: Allocated off-road parking directly behind the property.

properties.title: Horseshoe Barn, Knowle (near Crediton — stone barn conversion)
properties.body: Two-bedroom stone barn conversion (converted c.2011) in a rural farm courtyard near Knowle, 3 miles from Crediton. Vaulted ceilings, exposed roof timbers, oak flooring, open-plan living, rear garden with open countryside views. Off-street parking plus a double open-fronted car port. Freehold, with a modest management-committee contribution for the shared courtyard and treatment plant.
properties.why: The most genuine barn-conversion character and real parking (including a double car port) found anywhere this sweep — but Knowle is well inland near Crediton, a proper drive from both the coast and either brother, which is a real trade-off against your core brief. Included from Helmores' unique stock specifically because the character/parking combination is rare; weigh the distance honestly.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Genuine c.2011 stone barn conversion with vaulted ceilings and exposed roof timbers, plus a double open-fronted car port — a rare finished character+parking combination

properties.aiTake: The best barn-conversion character and real parking of the whole sweep, but Knowle is well inland near Crediton — a proper drive from the coast and both brothers, so the distance has to outweigh the character for this one to make sense.

parking.note: Off-street parking plus a double open-fronted car port — the best parking/character combination of the sweep, offset by the inland location.

properties.title: Church Stile Lane, Woodbury (Grade II cottage)
properties.body: Grade II listed one-double-bedroom cottage on Church Stile Lane in the heart of Woodbury village. Sitting room with exposed beams and fireplace, a flexible study space, a recently installed kitchen with integrated appliances, ground-floor bathroom, and a first-floor bedroom with a walk-in wardrobe. Enclosed rear garden with a patio and raised decking. Electric heating (no mains gas), fibre broadband available. Freehold, chain-free, Council Tax Band B.
properties.why: By far the cheapest genuinely characterful find of the whole search — a real Grade II cottage, freehold and chain-free, at less than half the price ceiling. It's right in Woodbury itself, so a brother is a short walk away and Woodbury Common's trails are effectively on the doorstep, same as the other two Woodbury cottages already on file, but this one is £100k+ cheaper than either. The trade-offs are size (just one double bedroom plus a study) and parking, which the listing confirms is on-street only rather than unstated.
properties.aiTake: The cheapest genuinely characterful property found all search — Grade II, freehold, chain-free, and close to a brother and the Common's trails — but it's compact (one double bedroom plus a study) with on-street parking only and a first-floor bedroom that rules out easy ground-floor adaptation.

research.question: Please do a deep dive: the roof looks a little bad on the back. Would love to find out about parking on this street — seems strangely empty in the photos but no off-street parking shown, what do they do? Also what's behind it — if I have my bike in the shed can I take it out of the back of the house behind that deck somewhere, or do I have to traipse the bike through the house? Check repair issues, permissions, and last sale prices/history.
research.findings: Roof: the listing text says nothing about roof condition either way — can't confirm or rule out what was spotted in the photos from the description alone. Genuinely needs eyes-on or a direct question to the agent, especially given Grade II status limits what repairs are straightforward/permitted. Parking: confirmed on-street only, nothing off-street mentioned anywhere in the listing. What's behind it / bike access: the listing only describes internal access — "the recently installed kitchen enjoys direct access to the garden" — with wooden steps up to a raised deck. No side gate, path, or external route is mentioned anywhere, and no shed/outbuilding is mentioned in the listing at all. Read at face value, a bike stored back there would go out through the kitchen, not around the side — but agents routinely leave out side-access details that exist in reality, so treat this as unconfirmed rather than definitively no side gate, until a viewing. Sale history: Land Registry has entries for other numbered addresses in the same cluster (2 Church Stile Cottages sold £270k in 2007; 3 Church Stile Cottages shows a logged 2026 sale of £657k, which looks like a likely data error given everything else on the lane — treat with caution) but nothing tying definitively to this exact cottage since the listing doesn't give a house number/name. Repairs/permissions: no planning applications, enforcement notices, or repair-related records found for this address — the only nearby planning activity on the lane is routine tree-pruning consent for 3 Church Stile Cottages, unrelated to structure or roof. Bottom line: nothing here rules it out, but the roof and rear-access questions genuinely can't be answered from the listing alone — worth putting directly to the agent before going further.

sources.label: Rightmove — Church Stile Lane sold prices
Zoopla — Church Stile Lane sold prices

parking.note: Confirmed on-street only by the listing itself, not just an omission — see the research findings below for more on this street specifically.

properties.title: Castle Lane, Exmouth (Grade II thatched cottage)
properties.body: Cob and thatched Grade II listed cottage, late 18th/early 19th century, in Littleham village — within Exmouth itself. One double bedroom, cosy sitting room with an open fireplace and exposed beams, dining room/study, modern fitted kitchen, shower room. Large private rear garden with flagstone patio, lawn, shrub borders, two sheds (one with power), plus a front garden. Thatch fully replaced in 2015, ridge replaced and thatch brushed in 2025. Freehold, no onward chain. Septic tank (£110/yr). Council Tax Band B. Pedestrian right of access exists for neighbouring cottages.
properties.why: A genuine thatched cottage — the thing Jesse said he loves most — and this one is actually inside Exmouth itself (Littleham), not a drive away in a separate village, which nothing else thatched on the list can claim. Freehold, no chain, well under budget, and the thatch has been properly maintained (redone 2015, ridge refreshed 2025 — not a looming repair bill). The catch: parking isn't mentioned at all (listing says ask agent), and it's not especially close to the coast, station, or trails despite being in Exmouth.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Two garden sheds, one with power — useful outbuilding space for a cottage this size, plus recently maintained thatch (2015/2025) rather than an ageing roof

properties.aiTake: A genuine thatched cottage right in Exmouth itself (Littleham) — freehold, no chain, well-maintained thatch, and a proper garden with two sheds — but parking isn't confirmed and it's not especially close to the coast, station, or trails despite being in town.

parking.note: Listing says "ask agent" — not confirmed either way.

properties.title: Mirey Lane, Woodbury (Grade II cottage)

flags.text: ON-STREET PARKING ONLY (+ free car park 2 min walk)

properties.body: Grade II listed mid-terrace cottage dating to the 1800s, right in the heart of Woodbury village. Open-plan lounge/diner with exposed wooden flooring and a character fireplace, fitted kitchen with solid wood units, two bedrooms upstairs with Victorian cast iron fireplaces. Front garden with a cobblestone wall, gate, pathway, herb and flower beds, and a cherry tree. No private rear garden, but the owner has a right of access across the rear courtyard garden to the adjoining lane. Freehold, no onward chain, gas central heating, Council Tax Band B. On-street parking plus a free short-stay car park (Flower Street) two minutes' walk away.
properties.why: A fourth Woodbury cottage now on file, and this one has the best-confirmed parking situation of any of them — on-street plus a genuine free short-stay car park just two minutes' walk away, not just an unstated gap. Real 1800s Grade II character (beams, cast iron fireplaces, chimney stack) and no chain. The trade-off is the garden: there's no true private plot, just rear courtyard access to the lane.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A genuine free short-stay car park two minutes' walk away — the best-confirmed parking situation of any Woodbury cottage on file, even without a dedicated space

properties.aiTake: The best-confirmed parking of any Woodbury cottage on file — on-street plus a free car park two minutes away — with genuine 1800s Grade II character, but no real private garden, just rear courtyard access. Nudged down slightly for price, since 'offers in excess of £250,000' likely lands past your real £170-200k target.

parking.note: On-street parking plus a genuine free short-stay car park (Flower Street) two minutes' walk away — better-confirmed than most "no parking info" entries.

properties.title: Longmeadow Road, Lympstone (Grade II thatched cottage — separate from the one-bed cottage already on file)
properties.body: Grade II listed thatched cottage on Longmeadow Road, Lympstone — beautifully presented, two double bedrooms. Sitting room with a gas wood burner, separate dining room, kitchen with utility area, modern family bathroom. Attractive lawned front garden, low-maintenance west-facing rear garden with a garden store. Gas central heating, freehold, no onward chain. Note: this is a different, larger, pricier property from the one-bed Longmeadow Road cottage already tracked (id: longmeadow, £250k, stream-side garden studio) — same street, unrelated listing.
properties.why: A genuine thatched cottage in Lympstone — the best-located village on the whole list, and Jesse's favourite roof type — but 48% over the £300k ceiling, well beyond the 'slightly over for something exceptional' bar the rest of the list holds to. Kept here as a benchmark/inspiration piece rather than a real contender, and flagged clearly as a separate property from the cheaper Longmeadow Road cottage already tracked so the two don't get confused.
properties.aiTake: A genuine thatched cottage in the best-located village on the list, but 48% over the £300k ceiling — kept as a benchmark, not a real contender at this price. Already well past the hard ceiling, on top of everything else.

parking.note: Not mentioned in the listing either way.

properties.title: Tythe Cottage Tea Rooms, Littleham Village (Grade II — AUCTION)

flags.text: AUCTION DATE UNCONFIRMED — verify with agent

properties.body: Grade II listed former tea rooms in Littleham village, within Exmouth — first time on the market in around 50 years. Ground floor: former restaurant/bar area and commercial kitchen. First floor: one-bedroom owner's accommodation (sitting room, bedroom, bathroom). Car parking to the rear of the building, two customer WCs. Needs sympathetic upgrading throughout. EPC C, freehold.
properties.why: A genuinely unusual find — a Grade II former tea rooms with living accommodation above, unlisted for half a century, going to auction with real rear parking (unusual for anything this close to Exmouth centre). Slightly over budget and needs work, but the character/rarity is real. Important caveat: the auction page shows a date of 11 December 2025, which has already passed — the lot appears to have been renumbered/relisted (322921, previously 21387) and other pages describe it as still open for bidding, so the current status and next auction date genuinely couldn't be pinned down this sweep. Treat as needing a direct call to the auctioneer before relying on any date shown.

checklist.uniqueFeature: Former tea rooms with a commercial kitchen and restaurant/bar space downstairs — unlisted for ~50 years, a genuinely oddball conversion opportunity

properties.aiTake: A genuinely unusual former-tea-rooms conversion with real rear parking, close to family in Exmouth — but it's over budget, needs work throughout, and the auction date/status couldn't be confirmed this sweep, so treat the timing as unreliable until you speak to the agent.

parking.note: Car parking to the rear of the building, confirmed in the listing.

properties.title: Victoria Road, Exmouth (ground-floor flat)
properties.body: One-bedroom ground-floor flat on Victoria Road, close to Exmouth town centre and seafront. Lounge with built-in storage, bedroom, kitchen with cupboards and freestanding cooker, three-piece bathroom, and a rear courtyard garden with access. Needs redecoration and modernisation throughout. Leasehold: 99 years from July 2007 (~80 years remaining), ground rent £291/year, service charge £4,078/year with major works planned for 2026. Council tax band A, EPC D.
properties.why: Submitted by family — cheap headline price and genuinely ground floor, but two things need weighing before getting excited: an offer of £58,000 has already been received on it, with only 5 working days from the listing's publication for anyone to beat it, so this may already be gone or about to go; and the £4,078/year service charge (with major works flagged for 2026 on top) would eat a huge chunk of the low purchase price over time. Family's own note flagged "no place to park, worrisome back roof" — parking isn't mentioned in the listing but Victoria Road has a residents' Zone B permit scheme for on-street parking; roof condition isn't addressed in the listing text either and would need a direct question to the agent or a viewing.
properties.aiTake: Cheapest thing on the whole list and genuinely ground-floor near the seafront, but a £58,000 offer already on the table and a punishing £4,078/year service charge (with major works pending) undercut the headline price badly — worth a very quick decision if pursued at all, not a leisurely one.

parking.note: Not mentioned in the listing. Victoria Road has Limited Waiting restrictions (Mon-Sat 8am-6pm, 1hr no return) with an exemption for Residents' Zone B permit holders — a resident's permit (around £35/year for a first permit) would be needed for reliable on-street parking rather than free unrestricted parking.

properties.title: Chapel Street, Sidbury (ground-floor flat)
properties.body: Purpose-built one-bedroom ground-floor flat in a small development in Sidbury village. Dual-aspect sitting/dining room overlooking communal gardens, modern kitchen with Corian worktops and built-in oven, double bedroom with built-in wardrobe, bathroom with electric shower. Communal gardens extend down to the River Sid with a walking path. Village shop, pub, primary school and bus links nearby. Leasehold, term expiring 24.03.2113 (~87 years remaining). Reduced 06/06/2026.
properties.why: The cheapest thing on the whole list at £140,000, and explicitly ground floor, but it's a real outlier geographically — Sidbury is inland near Sidmouth, further from both Woodbury Common and both brothers than anywhere else being tracked. Worth keeping on file purely as a rock-bottom-price, no-stairs option, but it's a fallback shape, not a first choice.
properties.aiTake: Rock-bottom price and genuinely ground-floor, which counts for a lot, but Sidbury is well off the beaten path relative to both the coast and the brothers, and there's no character or parking to speak of — a fallback option rather than a contender.

parking.note: Listing doesn't specify parking arrangements for this flat — worth asking the agent directly, since purpose-built developments like this sometimes have allocated bays not mentioned in the online listing.

properties.title: Bicton Street, Exmouth (Grade II townhouse + separate listed barn)

flags.text: HAS PARKING (off-road + rear space by barn)

properties.body: Substantial six-bedroom Grade II listed period townhouse over three floors in Exmouth, needing renovation, no onward chain. Two reception rooms, spacious kitchen/breakfast room, ground-floor bedroom with en-suite wet room, plus five further bedrooms across the upper floors. Wall-enclosed rear garden, gas central heating, solar water heating tubes. The standout feature: a separate Grade II listed brick barn (18' x 10') in the walled garden, suitable for a studio, workshop, or storage subject to consent. Off-road parking plus a rear space by the barn. Close to town centre, beach, and the railway station.
properties.why: Significantly over budget and needs real renovation work, but it's genuinely one of a kind — a Grade II townhouse with its own separate listed barn, right in Exmouth with off-road parking and no chain. The barn alone is the kind of oddball workshop/studio space Jesse likes flagged. Included as an exceptional/unique find despite the price, per the standing rule for unusual properties — but six bedrooms and renovation work make this a much bigger project than most of what's on the list.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A separate Grade II listed brick barn (18'x10') in the walled rear garden — usable as a studio, workshop, or storage subject to consent. A genuinely rare feature this close to Exmouth town centre.

properties.aiTake: A one-of-a-kind find — Grade II townhouse plus its own listed barn, in Exmouth with real off-road parking — but it's £115,000 over budget, needs renovation throughout, and six bedrooms is far more house than needed, so the price adjustment weighs it down despite the character.

parking.note: Off-road parking plus a rear parking space adjacent to the barn, confirmed in the listing.

properties.title: 1 Castle Cottages, Castle Lane, Woodbury (period cottage)
properties.body: Recently upgraded period cottage in a central Woodbury village location, no onward chain. Modern kitchen/dining room with utility, spacious sitting room, an office/4th-bedroom option, three double bedrooms (ensuite master), main bathroom. Private courtyard garden with a useful storage shed. Parking to the front. Electric boiler heating. Note: a neighbouring property has a Right of Way to access a drain located in the garden.
properties.why: A different Castle Lane cottage to the thatched one already on file, this one in Woodbury itself rather than Exmouth — genuinely close to a brother and the Common's trails, with real parking to the front (still not common on this list) and a storage shed. £75,000 over budget and not Grade II listed, but it's beautifully presented, no-chain, and move-in ready rather than needing work.
properties.aiTake: A genuinely nice, move-in-ready Woodbury cottage with real front parking and a storage shed, close to a brother and the Common — but £75,000 over budget and not particularly unusual architecturally, so it lands as a solid rather than standout option.

parking.note: "Parking to the front" listed as a key feature.

properties.title: Vicarage Road, East Budleigh (stone and cob period cottage)
properties.body: Stone and cob period cottage in East Budleigh with deceptively large accommodation. Ground floor: large living room, spacious kitchen/dining room, cloakroom, utility area. Upstairs: two bedrooms and a bathroom. South-facing, very secluded garden with well-fenced boundaries and a summerhouse (a neighbour has a right-of-way for access). Sits within the East Devon AONB, around 2 miles from the sea and 10 miles from Exeter. Freehold, parking included. Reduced 20/08/2025.
properties.why: A characterful stone-and-cob cottage with real parking and a genuinely private, secluded garden complete with a summerhouse — only £25,000 over budget, which is a modest stretch for what's on offer. East Budleigh keeps it close to the Otterton brother, and the AONB setting fits the rural-over-village-centre preference well.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A secluded south-facing garden with its own summerhouse

properties.aiTake: Genuine character, real parking, and an unusually private secluded garden with a summerhouse, close to the Otterton brother — only a modest £25,000 over budget, which makes this one of the more reasonable over-budget stretches on the list.

parking.note: Parking confirmed in the listing details, though exact arrangement (driveway vs allocated space) isn't spelled out.

properties.title: Cranes Lane, East Budleigh (renovated period cottage)
properties.body: Fully renovated terraced period cottage in East Budleigh. Entrance hall, sitting room, kitchen/dining room, cloakroom/WC, bathroom, two double bedrooms. Modern gas central heating and double glazing throughout. Attractive, low-maintenance courtyard garden with a wildlife pond and seating area. No allocated parking, but a public car park is around 130 yards away. Freehold. Added 21/02/2026.
properties.why: Unlike most of the character cottages on this list, this one is already fully renovated — modern heating, double glazing, nothing left to do. A wildlife pond in the courtyard garden is a nice unusual touch. The real drawback is parking: nothing allocated, just a public car park roughly 130 yards off, and it's £25,000 over budget.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A courtyard garden with its own wildlife pond

properties.aiTake: The one cottage on the list that's already fully renovated — nothing to do on move-in, plus a nice wildlife pond in the courtyard — but no allocated parking and £25,000 over budget pull it down despite the move-in-ready appeal.

parking.note: No allocated parking with the property. A public car park is roughly 130 yards away — realistically walkable but not guaranteed, and worth checking East Budleigh's car park capacity/cost before relying on it day-to-day.

properties.title: Greenway, Woodbury (Grade II listed cottage)
properties.body: Grade II listed period cottage in the heart of Woodbury village, arranged over three floors: sitting room with fireplace and alcove storage, kitchen/dining room, two bedrooms, plus a loft room with a vaulted ceiling and exposed beams (fitted with its own shower, usable as an occasional third bedroom or reception room). Front patio with lawn and a vegetable patch, a brick-built storage shed. On-street parking. Freehold, EPC C, council tax band B. Reduced on 13/05/2026.
properties.why: A second, distinct Greenway property from the one already tracked — this one is Grade II listed, spread over three floors with a genuinely useful loft room (vaulted ceiling, exposed beams, its own shower), and £30k under the hard budget ceiling. Right in Woodbury village centre, so a short walk from a brother and effectively on the doorstep of Woodbury Common. Same trade-off as most Woodbury-centre finds: on-street parking only, though the village has a free short-stay car park (Flower Street) a couple of minutes away if visitors need it.

checklist.uniqueFeature: A loft room with a vaulted ceiling, exposed beams and its own shower — usable as an occasional third bedroom

properties.aiTake: Grade II character, a genuinely useful loft room, freehold, and comfortably under budget right in Woodbury village — let down only by on-street-only parking, which is the standard trade-off for anything this central.

parking.note: On-street parking only, confirmed directly in the listing — no allocated space or garage. Woodbury village centre has a free short-stay car park on Flower Street a couple of minutes' walk away, as already established for other village-centre Woodbury entries.
